paper_metadata.title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations
paper_metadata.abstract: Increasing model size when pretraining natural language representations often results in improved performance on downstream tasks. However, at some point further model increases become harder due to GPU/TPU memory limitations and longer training times. To address these problems, we present two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase the training speed of BERT. Comprehensive empirical evidence shows that our proposed methods lead to models that scale much better compared to the original BERT. We also use a self-supervised loss that focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence, and show it consistently helps downstream tasks with multi-sentence inputs. As a result, our best model establishes new state-of-the-art results on the GLUE, RACE, and \squad benchmarks while having fewer parameters compared to BERT-large. The code and the pretrained models are available at https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT.

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces a parameter-efficient transformer model by using factorized embedding parameterization and cross-attention masking, enabling comparable performance to BERT with 1/18th the parameters.
extraction_data.application: Language understanding, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficiently learning language representations with fewer parameters
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT achieves large model performance with 60M parameters, 1/5th of BERT-large, using factorized embedding and cross-attention masking.
extraction_data.application: Language understanding, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficiently learning language representations with fewer parameters
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT-xlarge uses 235M parameters, achieving performance comparable to BERT-large (334M) with 70% fewer parameters, using factorized embedding and cross-attention masking.
extraction_data.application: Language understanding, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficiently learning language representations with fewer parameters
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT uses factorized embedding parameterization and cross-attention masking to reduce parameters while maintaining performance, achieving 128M parameters with comparable results to BERT's 117M.
extraction_data.application: Language understanding, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficiently learning language representations with fewer parameters
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces a lightweight BERT variant with reduced parameter count (1.5B) using factorized embedding parameterization and cross-attention, enabling efficient self-supervised learning for language understanding.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (not specified in paper, inferred from BERT-like training)
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficiently learning language representations with larger parameter counts while maintaining efficiency.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, and other NLP tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: An ensemble of ALBERT models (1M and 1.5M) trained on MLM and SOP tasks, achieving state-of-the-art performance on language understanding benchmarks.
extraction_data.application: Language understanding, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient language representation learning with reduced parameters.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised discriminative fine-tuning on specific tasks (e.g., RTE, STS, MRPC)
extraction_data.extension: The ensemble combines multiple ALBERT models trained with different parameters (1M and 1.5M) to improve performance on language understanding tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations
